title: Fix continuous audio playback (duration/timing bug)
description: Debug and fix track advancement logic. Issue: narration plays but music requires manual trigger instead of auto-continuing. Root cause: state.duration may be 0/undefined or race conditions in handleStateChange(). Test with both SDK device and external Spotify device.
notes:
## Research: Music Playback Best Practices (2026)

### Key Findings

**Spotify Web Playback SDK**
- Primary event: player_state_changed (fires at random intervals)
- Provides: duration_ms, position_ms, paused state
- Issue: duration not guaranteed to be available immediately

**Best Practice**: Event-driven + polling fallback
1. Listen to player_state_changed
2. Compare position vs duration to detect end
3. Poll every 1-2s if duration unavailable
4. External devices need polling via /v1/me/player API (your code already does this!)

**HTML5 Audio (for narration)**
- Use 'ended' event for reliable detection
- timeupdate fires 3-5x/sec (browser dependent)
- Safe spacing between clips: 500ms minimum

**Why Current Code Breaks**
- Timer: state.duration - state.position may be 0/undefined
- Position drift if user seeks
- External device state unreliable
- Race condition: multiple timers firing

**Solution Pattern**
Instead of one-shot timer, use event-driven with polling fallback.
This is what YouTube Music, Apple Music use.

Sources:
- Spotify Web Playback SDK Reference
- MDN: Audio for Web Games
- ISMIR 2017: Automatic Playlist Sequencing

## Implementation Plan (5 fixes, ~9 lines total in app.js)

Fix 1 (Root cause): NaN/zero duration guard in handleStateChange() + resumePlayback()
Fix 2: Move inNarration=false to just before spotifyPlay() — closes narration→music race
Fix 3: timerGen snapshot in setTimeout callbacks — prevents ghost advance after skip
Fix 4: Retry spotifyPlay() after 401 token refresh — token expiry resilience
Fix 5: Post-fetch generation check in startExternalPoll() — external poll skip race

All changes in: public/app.js

## Additional requirements from user review

Fix 6: Remove browser SpeechSynthesis fallback entirely — produces horrible robotic voice, not acceptable UX
  - When Fish Audio fails → skip narration, go straight to spotifyPlay()
  - No browser TTS under any circumstances

Test coverage must include:
- Skip (▶▶) AND Previous (◀◀) buttons
- Clicking track titles in the playlist
- Pause/resume at all stages

## Implementation complete (2026-04-25)

All 6 fixes applied to public/app.js — net -12 lines.

Verified in place:
- handleStateChange (lines 332-340): duration guard + remaining<=500 guard + timerGen
- resumePlayback (lines 384-392): same guards in SDK resume path
- playStep ended handler (lines 535-536): inNarration=false moved to immediately before spotifyPlay()
- playStep catch handler (lines 538-543): speechSynthesis fallback removed
- playStep else branch (lines 544-546): speechSynthesis branch removed
- spotifyPlay (lines 665-669): 401 handler retries after refresh
- startExternalPoll (line 624): post-fetch generation check added

Status: awaiting end-to-end browser verification by user.
close_reason: Fixed 5 playback bugs + removed browser TTS fallback in app.js: duration guard, inNarration race fix, timerGen ghost-advance prevention, 401 token retry, external poll generation check

title: Playback broken after save-playlist work: Widevine DRM 400
description:
Playback was working before the save-playlist feature was added. Now after narration ends, the Spotify track does not play.

SYMPTOMS:
- Narration (TTS audio) plays fine
- [spotify] play response: 204 — play command is accepted by Spotify
- Immediately after: POST /v1/widevine-license/v1/audio/license 400 (Bad Request) repeats ~6 times
- Audio never starts

KEY OBSERVATION: The 204 means the API call succeeded. The failure is inside spotify-player.js (the Spotify Web Playback SDK) during DRM decryption — not in our code.

LIKELY CAUSE: The SDK captures spotifyToken at init time via getOAuthToken: cb => cb(spotifyToken). During the save-playlist flow, refreshTokenIfNeeded() may have updated spotifyToken, causing a mismatch between the token the SDK was initialized with and the one now in scope. When the SDK tries to get a Widevine license using the stale/rotated token, Spotify rejects it with 400.

INVESTIGATE:
1. Does re-initializing the SDK (initSpotifySDK) after a token refresh fix it?
2. Does the Widevine 400 happen when no save was performed in the same session?
3. Check if spotifyToken is being mutated between SDK init and playback

RELATED: save-playlist feature added in epic literary-guide-vh5 — refreshTokenIfNeeded() is now called from saveToSpotify() which runs concurrently with the playback flow.

title: Add Save to Spotify button to UI
description: index.html + app.js: Add 'Save to Spotify' button that appears once playlist is resolved and has at least 1 matched URI. States: default → loading (spinner) → saved (check + 'Open in Spotify' link). Disable if 0 URIs matched with tooltip 'No tracks matched on Spotify'.

title: Implement saveToSpotify() in app.js
description: Client-side function that: 1) GET /v1/me to get user ID, 2) POST /v1/users/{id}/playlists with name=playlistTitle, public=true, description='Generated by Literary Guide. X of Y tracks matched on Spotify.', 3) POST /v1/playlists/{id}/tracks with matched URIs (batch in chunks of 100 if needed). Uses existing spotifyToken.

title: Add playlist-modify-public OAuth scope
description: In app.js loginSpotify(), add 'playlist-modify-public' to the scope param in the Spotify authorization URL. Required for creating public playlists.

title: Extract playlist title from Murakami Radio scraper
description: scraper.js: extract the article title (h1 or leading heading) from the Murakami Radio page alongside rawText. server.js: store as job.playlistTitle and expose it in /api/status/:jobId response. For book uploads, derive playlistTitle from the uploaded filename (strip extension).

title: [epic] Save playlist to Spotify
description: Allow users to save the generated literary playlist directly to their Spotify account as a public playlist. Covers OAuth scope update, playlist title extraction, API calls, and UI.

title: Implement caching for Murakami radio episodes
description: Pre-generate and cache ~80 episodes of Murakami radio. Store generated links and descriptions. Avoid regenerating when cache is cleared.

title: Complete Spotify integration
description: Refine Spotify Web Playback SDK integration. Ensure reliable device detection, token refresh, and external device handling. Add error recovery for playback failures.

title: Literary Guide Audio Playback & Integration
description: Fix continuous audio playback, add Spotify integration, implement caching for Murakami radio episodes
